reason: Both correctly land on "fix it" and cite the ~2.2 LTV:CAC and ~6-month payback as marginal-but-workable. But Answer A is substantially more useful: it gives concrete, prioritized fixes (negative keywords/search terms report, campaign concentration on converting keywords, tracking optimization to revenue rather than leads, LTV-side levers) and a concrete kill trigger with alternative channels (SEO, outbound, partnerships) for reallocation — hitting most of the key points (negative keywords, budget/intent concentration, payback framing, channel comparison). Answer B is accurate but very thin, essentially restating the decision with only "cut worst-converting keywords/geos and tighten landing pages" as the actionable content — it misses negative keywords, tracking-to-revenue, blended CAC, and channel-comparison points entirely. Neither explicitly separates branded vs. generic, but A's greater actionable specificity makes it more decision-useful without padding — each point is a real, applicable step.
